action.reasoning: Observation: The previous evaluation yielded a negative IC and only 62 days were evaluated instead of ~480. Analysis: The low number of days was caused by `np.std(p_vals) == 0` failing to catch very small standard deviations (due to float precision), leading to NaNs in Pearson correlation which caused those days to be skipped. The negative IC suggests the model hyperparameters might be suboptimal (too much regularization). Decision: Update `predict.py` to use `np.std(p_vals) < 1e-8` to properly catch near-zero standard deviations. Update `train.py` to use more standard LightGBM hyperparameters for stock prediction (lower regularization, smaller depth). Run local test and submit for evaluation.
